zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabelle de Aragon reine de France於1247年8月19日出生在法國藍多-盧希隆賀勞爾特蒙培利也時，她的父親King Jacme de Aragón I39歲，母親Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán33歲。她於1262年7月6日與Philippe III le Hardi roi de France在法國奧維普-德-o結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1271年1月28日在義大利卡拉布里亞科森扎享年23歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabelle de Aragon reine de France于1247年8月19日出生在法国藍多-盧希隆賀勞爾特蒙培利也时，她的父亲King Jacme de Aragón I39岁，母亲Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán33岁。她于1262年7月6日与Philippe III le Hardi roi de France在法国奧維普-德-o结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1271年1月28日在意大利卡拉布里亞科森扎享年23岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aragon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Ruben, Fernando, Jesus, Julio, Luis, Raul, Mario, Alfonso.

Spanish (Aragón) and southern French: habitational name from Aragon, Spain, which was an independent kingdom from 1035 to 1479. It took its name from the river Aragón, which arises in its northwestern corner. The river name may be related to Basque (h)ara(n) ‘valley’; in Basque, Aragon is called Aragoa or Aragoi, which probably means ‘high valley’. Compare Deragon .

variants.name: Deragon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Monique, Pierre, Raoul, Rejean.

French: variant of Daragon, habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from Aragon, a region and medieval kingdom in Spain (see Aragon ), or a habitational name for someone from Aragon, the name of several places in various parts of France. The surname Deragon is very rare in France. Compare Dragon .

variants.name: Aragones
meanings: Spanish (Aragonés): habitational or ethnic name for someone from Aragon (see Aragon ).

variants.name: Aragona
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Ciro, Domenic, Domenico, Gaspare.  Spanish Carolina, Ninfa, Rosario.

Italian (mainly southern): habitational or ethnic name for someone from the former kingdom of Aragon (see   Aragon  ); the surname arrived in Italy in the wake of the Aragonese conquest and domination of southern Italy, which began in 1282.

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabelle de Aragon reine de France was born on 19 August 1247, in Montpellier, Hérault, Languedoc-Roussillon, France, her father, King Jacme de Aragón I, was 39 and her mother, Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán, was 33. She married Philippe III le Hardi roi de France on 6 July 1262, in Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 28 January 1271, in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy, at the age of 23, and was buried in Paris, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabelle de Aragon reine de France est née le 19 août 1247, à Montpellier, Hérault, Languedoc-Roussillon, France, son père, King Jacme de Aragón I, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Philippe III le Hardi roi de France le 6 juillet 1262, à Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée le 28 janvier 1271, à Cosenza, Calabria, Italie, à l’âge de 23 ans, et a été enterrée à Paris, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabelle de Aragon reine de France am 19. August 1247 in Montpellier, Hérault, Languedoc-Roussillon, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater King Jacme de Aragón I 39 und ihre Mutter Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán 33. Sie hat am 6. Juli 1262 in Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Frankreich Philippe III le Hardi roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 28. Januar 1271 im Alter von 23 Jahren in Cosenza, Calabria, Italien und wurde in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabelle de Aragon reine de France è nata il il 19 agosto 1247 nel Montpellier, Hérault, Languedoc-Roussillon, Francia, sua padre, King Jacme de Aragón I, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Philippe III le Hardi roi de France il 6 luglio 1262, nel Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 28 gennaio 1271, nel Cosenza, Calabria, Italia, all'età di 23 anni, e fu sepolta a Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabelle de Aragon reine de Franceが1247年8月19日にフランスラングエドク-ロウシロンheラウルトモントペリールで生まれた時，父King Jacme de Aragón Iは39歳，母Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolánは33歳であった。1262年7月6日にフランスアウバーグンプウイ-デ-ドoメでPhilippe III le Hardi roi de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1271年1月28日にイタリアカラブリアコセンザで23歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabelle de Aragon reine de France(이)가 1247년 8월 19일 프랑스 랭도c-루시런 he라트 몽펠리어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 King Jacme de Aragón I(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1262년 7월 6일 프랑스 오버그니 푸이-데-드o에서 Philippe III le Hardi roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1271년 1월 28일 이탈리아 캘라브리아 코센자에서 23세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에 묻혔다.

mn.19 August 1247–28 January 1271: 1247 оны Наймдугаар сарын 19–1271 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 28

pt.19 August 1247–28 January 1271: 19 de agosto de 1247–28 de janeiro de 1271
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabelle de Aragon reine de France nasceu em 19 de agosto de 1247, em Montpellier, Hérault, Languedoc-Roussillon, França, seu pai, King Jacme de Aragón I, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Philippe III le Hardi roi de France em 6 de julho de 1262, em Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 28 de janeiro de 1271, em Cosenza, Calabria, Itália, com 23 anos, e foi sepultada em Paris, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabelle de Aragon reine de France родилась 19 августа 1247 г. в местности монтпеллиир, хeраулт, лангуедок-руссиллон, Франция, её отцу по имени King Jacme de Aragón I было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philippe III le Hardi roi de France 6 июля 1262 г. в местности пуи-де-дoм, аувиргн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 28 января 1271 г. в местности косенза, калабря, Италия в возрасте 23 лет и была похоронена в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.19 August 1247–28 January 1271: 19 de agosto de 1247–28 de enero de 1271
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabelle de Aragon reine de France nació el 19 de agosto de 1247, en Montpellier, Hérault, Languedoc-Roussillon, Francia, su papá, King Jacme de Aragón I, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Reina de Aragón Árpád-házi Jolán, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Philippe III le Hardi roi de France el 6 de julio de 1262, en Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 28 de enero de 1271, en Cosenza, Calabria, Italia, a la edad de 23 años, y fue sepultada en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.
